title: The opportunity I almost said no to
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining the hesitation pattern and what it has cost.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 35 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for tracing what happens between a good opportunity arriving and your answer — and where the delay comes from.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 35 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to trace the hesitation pattern — what it feels like, where it lives, and what it costs.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 35 of the MONEY program. The delay between a good opportunity arriving and a confident yes is not neutral time: something happens in it. These prompts look at what.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Think of a time a good opportunity arrived and you hesitated. What was the hesitation?
Write the specific opportunity and the specific hesitation — not a general pattern but that one occasion.
Did you eventually say yes or no? What tipped it?
Write what happened. If you said yes, what shifted? If you said no, what won?
What did that decision bring — or cost?
Write what followed. Not dramatised — just what actually happened because of the yes or the no.
Is there an opportunity I am hesitating on right now?
Write it specifically. What is the opportunity? What is the hesitation saying?
What would I do if I trusted that I was ready enough?
Write the action. Not the version where you are perfectly prepared — the version where you are ready enough.
Adapted from Day 35 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
